name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-33
title: Extend rabbit_subline with optional icon parameter; wire active-flag warnings in session-init.py to use it
description:
UX improvement: at SessionStart, active runtime-flag warnings currently render in the same plain subline format as informational policy-context lines. They're operationally much more important but visually identical. Result: operator's eye sweeps past 'HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE' with the same weight as 'philosophy.md — machine-first; bounded scope'.

After this change, three visual tiers communicate hierarchy at SessionStart:
  - Welcome banner (bars + double icon, unchanged)
  - Active-flag warnings: '[(rabbit-emoji) rabbit (rabbit-emoji)] ICON content' (gain leading icon)
  - Policy info sublines: '[(rabbit-emoji) rabbit (rabbit-emoji)] content' (unchanged)

Sample output after change:
  [(brand)] (welcome-banner-with-bars)
  [(brand)] philosophy.md   — machine-first; bounded scope; designed deprecation
  [(brand)] spec-rules.md   — determinism first; schema contracts; lifecycle ownership
  [(brand)] coding-rules.md — think first; simplicity; surgical edits; goal-driven TDD
  [(brand)] (key-emoji) HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE — Step 4 skipped...
  [(brand)] (siren-emoji) BYPASS-PERMISSIONS MODE ACTIVE — Claude Code native per-write prompts skipped...

RATIONALE:
- Icon-vs-no-icon at subline level carries semantic value: icon = alert, no-icon = info
- Eye is drawn to icons; warnings now stand out from policy lines
- Welcome still gets top visual billing (banner format)
- sync-check.py (Stop hook) warnings already display icons via the rabbit_print banner path (icon kept after CONTRACT-BACKLOG-32 simplification); this change brings SessionStart sublines into closer parity (still distinct: SessionStart uses subline form without bars; Stop uses compact banner form with bars dropped per CONTRACT-32 simplification — wait, CONTRACT-32 kept the icon and dropped only the bars, so Stop renders '[brand] icon content' too. After this change, SessionStart subline warnings also render '[brand] icon content'. The two contexts now render warnings identically.)

SCOPE OF CHANGE:

1. .claude/features/contract/scripts/rabbit_print.py
   - Extend rabbit_subline signature: add optional icon parameter
     def rabbit_subline(text: str, color: str = 'green', icon: str = None) -> str
   - When icon is None (default): unchanged behavior — '{ansi}{brand} {text}{reset}'
   - When icon is provided: '{ansi}{brand} {icon} {text}{reset}'
   - Backwards-compatible (existing callers pass no icon, no behavior change)

2. .claude/features/contract/schemas/rabbit-print-messages.json
   - No change needed (the icon field per message-id already exists from prior cycles)

3. .claude/features/contract/docs/spec/spec.md
   - Update rabbit_subline signature/contract description (the invariant covering rabbit_print module — number shifted by ws36 renumber; grep for 'rabbit_subline' to find current Inv)

4. .claude/features/rabbit-cage/hooks/session-init.py
   - In the active_flags loop (around line 168-169), change:
     lines.append(rabbit_subline(f'{flag["body"]} (revoke: {flag["revoke"]})'))
   - To:
     lines.append(rabbit_subline(f'{flag["body"]} (revoke: {flag["revoke"]})', color='red', icon=flag['icon']))
   - The icon field on each flag dict is already populated from _runtime_flags.active_flags() (which pulls from the rabbit-print-messages.json registry entries for human-approval-bypass + bypass-permissions-active). Verify that mapping exists; if not, add it.

5. Tests
   - .claude/features/contract/test/test-rabbit-print-renderer.py: add test for rabbit_subline with icon parameter
   - .claude/features/rabbit-cage/test/test-backlog-27-runtime-flags-display.py: update assertions to expect the new format (leading icon) in session-init output

6. Rebuild deployed copies via build.py.

NO CHANGES TO:
- Welcome banner (unchanged)
- Policy info sublines (unchanged — they don't pass an icon)
- sync-check.py (its warnings already display icons via the named wrappers + rabbit_print banner path)
- The icon registry entries (already exist)
- The marker file naming
- The flag-body text content

NO COUPLING TO PENDING WORK:
- Independent of RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-31 (bypass-X rename)
- Independent of any other open backlog
- Can land in any small follow-up workspace

Acceptance:
- rabbit_subline accepts optional icon parameter
- session-init.py warnings render with leading icon
- Policy sublines unchanged
- Welcome unchanged
- All tests pass

history.note: TDD cycle complete (secondary item resolved by same commit)
